pattern: Find the middle with slow/fast pointers, reverse the second half in place, then compare it against the first half to decide the palindrome in constant extra space.
hints: A list is a palindrome when its values read the same front-to-back as back-to-front; the simple route is to copy the values out and check, but you can do better.
Use a slow and a fast pointer to land on the middle, then reverse the second half of the list so you can walk inward from both ends.

After reversing the back half, advance one pointer from the head and one from the new tail, comparing values; they match all the way only if the list is a palindrome (restore the list afterward if you want to be tidy).

edge_inputs.description: single node is always a palindrome
odd-length palindrome ignores the exact middle
differs only at the very ends

stress.description: construct a guaranteed palindrome from a random half, so the true case is hit at length
stress.note: deterministically a palindrome so the in-place reversal logic is exercised end to end

stress.description: random list, usually not a palindrome, to exercise the negative path
